docs.title: Welcome to OmniOCR
docs.text: OmniOCR is an open-source, multilingual OCR toolkit designed to handle images, PDFs, subtitles, and more — all with offline-first capabilities and optional AI-enhanced postprocessing.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Multilingual OCR (100+ languages)
  • OCR Engines: Tesseract + EasyOCR
  • Image, PDF, and Subtitle (SRT/SUB) support
  • Offline-first, no cloud dependency
  • AI-Powered post-correction with BERT
  • Web UI, CLI, REST API

docs.title: Why OmniOCR?
docs.text:
• Easy to use and extend
  • Fully modular and testable
  • Designed for real-world documents, screenshots, and scanned pages
  • Built-in subtitle parsing and OCR

docs.title: Get Started
docs.text:
To begin, see Getting Started or jump directly into:

  • Command Line Interface
  • REST API
  • Frequently Asked Questions

MIT License | Made with ♥ by GeekNeuron

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
=== [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md] ===

OmniOCR – System Architecture

Overview

OmniOCR is built on a modular, extensible pipeline with multiple OCR engines, AI-based correction, and multi-platform UI delivery.

🔧 Pipeline Flow

flowchart TD A[Input: Image/PDF/Subtitle] --> B[Preprocessor] B --> C[OCR Engine] C --> D[Language Detector] D --> E[AI Postprocessor (ParsBERT)] E --> F[Output Formatter] F --> G[CLI/API/GUI]

🧱 Module Breakdown

core/

ocr_engine.py: Wraps Tesseract, EasyOCR

preprocessor.py: Noise reduction, thresholding

postprocessor.py: Rule-based text fix

ai/

lang_detect.py: Fast langdetect wrapper

post_correction.py: Transformer-based fixer

interface/

api.py: FastAPI endpoints

ui_streamlit.py: Streamlit + Material CSS

desktop/

PySide6 + QSS theming (Material)

mobile/composeApp/

Compose Multiplatform Android/iOS/Desktop

Uses shared Kotlin logic with Material 3

omniocr/

CLI using Typer

Entrypoint: main.py

📡 Supported Inputs/Outputs

Input Output

PNG, JPG TXT, JSON PDF TXT, PDF SUB/IDX SRT, TXT EPUB TXT

🧩 Extensibility

Add OCR engines easily in ocr_engine.py

Add postprocessing layers in post_correction.py

Add new input types via core/parser

REST + WebSocket ready

🗺️ Architecture Benefits

Clean separation of concerns

Asynchronous-ready backend

UI-agnostic processing engine

Maintained by @GeekNeuron

docs.title: OmniOCR – Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Modular architecture with support for:
  • Tesseract OCR
  • EasyOCR
  • FastAPI + Streamlit + CLI interface
  • Material Design GUI (Desktop via PySide6, Mobile via Compose)
  • ParsBERT integration for Persian post-correction
  • Input format support: Image, PDF, Subtitle, EPUB
  • Multilingual detection with langdetect
  • Full cross-platform structure (Linux, macOS, Windows, Android, iOS)

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • NA

docs.title: Removed
docs.text:
• NA

Maintained by @GeekNeuron

docs.title: OmniOCR – References & Citations
docs.text: This document provides citations for all external models, datasets, and academic resources used or referenced in OmniOCR.

docs.title: Tesseract
docs.text: Smith, R. (2007). An overview of the Tesseract OCR engine. In Ninth International Conference on Document Analysis and Recognition (ICDAR 2007) (Vol. 2, pp. 629-633). IEEE.

docs.title: EasyOCR
docs.text: JaidedAI. (2020). EasyOCR: Ready-to-use OCR with 80+ Languages Supported. https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR

docs.title: TrOCR (Planned)
docs.text: Li, M., Chen, Y., Yu, W., Shi, S., & Liu, G. (2021). TrOCR: Transformer-based Optical Character Recognition with Pre-trained Models. arXiv preprint arXiv:2109.10282.

docs.title: ParsBERT
docs.text: Farahani, M., Gharachorloo, M., Farahani, M., & Manthouri, M. (2020). ParsBERT: Transformer-based Model for Persian Language Understanding. arXiv preprint arXiv:2005.12515.

docs.title: Langdetect
docs.text: Nakatani, S. (2010). Language Detection Library for Java. https://github.com/shuyo/language-detection

docs.title: Datasets
docs.text:
Several training and evaluation datasets may be used or cited: - Persian-OCR Dataset - Farsi Handwriting Dataset

For academic use, please cite the original papers above. Maintained by @GeekNeuron

docs.title: OmniOCR – Supported Datasets
docs.text: This document lists sample and benchmark datasets that OmniOCR supports or recommends for evaluation and training.

docs.title: Persian OCR
docs.text:
• Persian_OCR
  • Source: https://github.com/mohammadpz/Persian_OCR
  • Content: Printed Persian text in scanned images

  • Format: JPG + TXT

  • FarsiHandwriting

  • Source: https://github.com/amir-saniyan/FarsiHandwriting
  • Content: Handwritten Persian words
  • Format: PNG + CSV

docs.title: 🌍 Multilingual OCR
docs.text:
• IIIT-5K Words Dataset
  • Source: https://cvit.iiit.ac.in/research/projects/cvit-projects/the-iiit-5k-word-dataset

  • Format: Cropped word images

  • SynthText

  • Source: https://github.com/ankush-me/SynthText
  • Content: Synthetic scene text in English
  • Format: Image + annotation

docs.title: 📊 Dataset Usage in OmniOCR
docs.text:
• All datasets can be used for:
  • Performance benchmarking
  • Postprocessing evaluation
  • Fine-tuning language models

docs.title: 📝 Notes
docs.text:
• OmniOCR doesn’t redistribute datasets.
  • Please refer to original license for each.

Maintained by @GeekNeuron

docs.title: OmniOCR – Models & Engines
docs.text: This document clarifies the OCR and AI models used in OmniOCR, their sources, and licensing.

docs.title: 1. Tesseract OCR
docs.text:
• Source: https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract
  • Version: 5.3+
  • Languages: 100+ (incl. fa, ar, en, zh, ja)
  • License: Apache 2.0

docs.title: 2. EasyOCR
docs.text:
• Source: https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR
  • Framework: PyTorch
  • Languages: 80+
  • License: Apache 2.0

docs.title: 3. Planned: TrOCR (Microsoft)
docs.text:
• Model: microsoft/trocr-base-stage1
  • HuggingFace: https://huggingface.co/microsoft/trocr-base-stage1
  • License: MIT
  • Usage: Optical character transformer for better Arabic/Farsi accuracy

docs.title: 1. ParsBERT
docs.text:
• Source: https://huggingface.co/HooshvareLab/bert-base-parsbert-uncased
  • Type: Transformer language model for Persian
  • Purpose: Post-OCR error correction
  • License: Apache 2.0

docs.title: 2. Langdetect
docs.text:
• Purpose: Language auto-detection
  • Notes: Statistical, non-ML; supports 55+ langs

docs.title: Future Model Integration
docs.text:
Task Model Suggestion Source Layout OCR LayoutLMv3, Donut HuggingFace / NAVER / Microsoft Table Extraction CascadeTabNet, PaddleOCR GitHub OCR QA Correction BERT QA, FiD, T5 QA HuggingFace Post-OCR Summarize BART, Pegasus, T5-small Google, Facebook AI

This document is updated as models evolve. Maintained by @GeekNeuron

docs.title: About OmniOCR
docs.text: OmniOCR is a modular, offline-first OCR platform designed to unify OCR workflows across languages, file types, and interfaces. Built by GeekNeuron with the goal of making OCR accessible, customizable, and intelligent.

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Support real-world OCR tasks: images, scans, subtitles, and PDFs
  • Combine traditional OCR engines (Tesseract) with deep learning (EasyOCR, BERT)
  • Offer multiple interfaces: CLI, Web, REST API
  • Prioritize speed, offline support, and language extensibility

docs.title: Maintainers
docs.text:
• GeekNeuron — creator & lead developer
  • Contributors

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • Tesseract / EasyOCR
  • FastAPI, Typer, Streamlit
  • HuggingFace Transformers (for AI postprocessing)
  • Docker, Pytest, GitHub Actions

docs.title: License
docs.text:
This project is licensed under the MIT License.

Want to help? Read the Contributing Guide!

docs.title: OmniOCR provides a simple REST API via FastAPI.
Run the API
docs.text:
uvicorn OmniOCR/interface/api:app --reload

Then visit:

http://localhost:8000/docs

docs.title: Available Endpoints
docs.text: POST /ocr/image

docs.title: OCR a base64-encoded image.
docs.text:
{
  "image": "<base64 string>",
  "lang": "fas+eng",
  "engine": "tesseract"
}

docs.title: Returns:
docs.text:
{
  "text": "...",
  "lang": "fa",
  "confidence": 0.92
}

POST /ocr/pdf

docs.title: OCR all pages of a base64-encoded PDF file.
docs.text: POST /ocr/subtitle

docs.title: OCR a subtitle file uploaded as form-data.
Available at:
docs.text: http://localhost:8000/docs

docs.title: OpenAPI schema:
docs.text: http://localhost:8000/openapi.json

docs.title: Command Line Interface (CLI)
docs.text: OmniOCR includes a full-featured CLI powered by Typer.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text: omniocr ocr path/to/image.jpg --lang fas

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text:
ocr

Run OCR on a file or folder.

omniocr ocr input.jpg --lang fas+eng --engine tesseract

docs.title: Options:
docs.text:
--engine: tesseract or easyocr

--lang: Language code(s) (e.g. fas, eng, fas+eng)

--out: Output file name (default: ocr_output.txt)

--format: Output format (txt/json/pdf)

docs.title: subtitle
docs.text:
OCR for subtitle files (.srt, .sub).

omniocr subtitle movie.srt --lang eng --out results.txt

docs.title: pdf
docs.text:
Run OCR on all pages of a PDF.

omniocr pdf document.pdf --lang fas --engine tesseract

docs.title: Batch OCR
docs.text:
Supports processing of folders:

omniocr ocr ./folder/ --lang fas --out results/

docs.title: Help
docs.text: omniocr --help

docs.title: ❓ What formats are supported?
docs.text:
• .jpg, .png, .tif
  • .pdf
  • .srt, .sub
  • .epub

docs.title: ❓ Can I use OmniOCR offline?
docs.text: Yes. OmniOCR is offline-first. Internet is required only for model downloads (optional).

docs.title: ❓ Which OCR engines are available?
docs.text:
• tesseract
  • easyocr

docs.title: ❓ Which languages are supported?
docs.text: Over 100+ via Tesseract, including: - Farsi (fa), English (en), Arabic (ar), Turkish (tr), Chinese (zh), Japanese (ja)

docs.title: ❓ How do I run a batch OCR on folder?
docs.text: omniocr ocr ./images/ --lang fas --engine easyocr

docs.title: ❓ How to export output to JSON or PDF?
docs.text:
Use the --format flag:

omniocr ocr input.jpg --format json

docs.title: Still have questions? Open an issue.
Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to OmniOCR! This guide helps you set up and run your first OCR job quickly.

docs.title: 1. Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.8+
  • pip
  • Optional: Tesseract OCR engine

Install dependencies:

python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

Install Tesseract (if using it):

# Ubuntu

sudo apt install tesseract-ocr tesseract-ocr-fas

# macOS (Homebrew)
brew install tesseract

docs.title: CLI:
docs.text: omniocr ocr path/to/image.jpg --lang fas+eng

docs.title: Web UI:
docs.text: streamlit run OmniOCR/interface/web_ui.py

docs.title: REST API:
docs.text: uvicorn OmniOCR/interface/api:app --reload

docs.title: Run OCR on an image:
docs.text: omniocr ocr samples/scan.jpg --lang fas --engine tesseract

docs.title: OCR from a subtitle:
docs.text: omniocr subtitle samples/sample.srt --lang eng

docs.title: Continue to CLI Usage or API.
